reasoning: This animation sequence will show the robot expressing caution or warning with the downward-pointing triangle.  The movement will involve bringing both arms up in a gesture that mimics pointing downward, then slowly returning to a neutral position. This conveys a sense of controlled warning or focused attention, rather than a sudden, abrupt movement. The sequence will use a total of 4 frames. Frame 1: Both arms start slightly out to the sides at shoulder height, palms facing down. Frame 2: Both arms lower slightly, pivoting at the shoulder inward and bending at the wrist, mimicking a downward-pointing gesture. Frame 3: The arms return to the shoulder height, but now they are angled more toward the body and wrists are more flat. Frame 4: The arms return to the neutral position at the sides.
